primary_sources.finding: CloudEvents separates an occurrence from its event record, requires source-plus-id uniqueness, and permits extension attributes for correlation metadata. A received event is a record of an occurrence, not proof of later downstream occurrences.
Trace context supplies interoperable correlation identifiers; correlation identity does not establish epistemic truth or completion.

finding: Peer coordination needs a contiguous, evidence-bearing receipt chain. Trigger intent, control-plane acceptance, observed execution, committed output, and verified completion are distinct occurrences and must not be collapsed.
design_implication: Use one correlation identifier across append-only receipts, require exact next-state transitions, bind each receipt to its predecessor by SHA-256, and require state-specific evidence before advancing. Completion is true only at peer_completion_verified.

static_checks: validator requires the chain to begin at trigger_intent_recorded
only contiguous state transitions are accepted
every receipt shares the chain correlation_id
every noninitial receipt binds the canonical predecessor bytes by SHA-256
complete is true only for peer_completion_verified
verification.runtime_limit: No repository execution shell or completed CI run was available in this run; test passage is not claimed.

uncertainty: Canonical JSON serialization is implementation-local rather than an external canonicalization standard.
Digest linkage detects mutation only when a trusted copy of the terminal digest is retained.
The module validates evidence presence, not the truth or authority of evidence values.
Replay, signatures, scheduler observation, and commit ancestry remain separate verification layers.
falsification_route: Run node --test tools/frontier-research/trigger-receipt-chain.test.mjs in a clean checkout.
Attempt skipped-state, correlation-substitution, duplicate-receipt, broken-link, and missing-evidence inputs.
Integrate a real peer trigger and show that scheduler acceptance alone leaves complete=false.
Require independent verification of run identity, commit existence, output paths, and acceptance criteria before the terminal receipt.
privacy_boundary: Contains public standards, public repository paths, synthetic identifiers, and abstract team coordination only; no private chat, health, credentials, or identifying personal data.
